Performances and Profiling¶

Performance Analysis¶

This section presents the comprehensive performance analysis of the neural network interpolator across different dataset sizes.

Benchmark Results¶

The model was tested on three dataset sizes (1K, 5K, 10K samples) with the following configuration: - Hidden layers: [64, 32, 16] - Learning rate: 0.001 - Max epochs: 50 - Optimizer: Adam (via scikit-learn MLPRegressor)

Performance Metrics Table¶

Size

Training Time

Memory Usage

MSE

R²

1,000

0.054 s

0.88 MB

0.0116

0.9734

5,000

0.142 s

0.16 MB

0.0108

0.9745

10,000

0.217 s

0.11 MB

0.0101

0.9762

Key Findings¶

Scalability:

  • Training Time: Nearly linear scaling observed (~0.05s per 1K samples)

    • 1K → 5K: 2.6x increase in time for 5x data

    • 5K → 10K: 1.5x increase in time for 2x data

    • This demonstrates efficient scaling behavior for CPU-based training

  • Memory Usage: Extremely efficient memory footprint

    • Memory delta remains under 1 MB for all dataset sizes

    • Slight decrease in delta for larger datasets suggests efficient batch processing

    • Total memory consumption well within typical system constraints

Model Performance:

  • Accuracy (R²): Consistent improvement with dataset size

    • 1K samples: R² = 0.9734 (97.34% variance explained)

    • 5K samples: R² = 0.9745

    • 10K samples: R² = 0.9762

    • Demonstrates the model effectively learns the underlying 5D→1D mapping

  • Mean Squared Error: Decreases with more training data

    • 1K: MSE = 0.0116

    • 5K: MSE = 0.0108 (7% improvement)

    • 10K: MSE = 0.0101 (13% improvement overall)

Conclusions¶

The MLPRegressor-based interpolator demonstrates:

  1. Excellent scalability: Sub-second training even for 10K samples on CPU

  2. Memory efficiency: Minimal memory footprint suitable for resource-constrained environments

  3. Strong performance: >97% R² across all dataset sizes, meeting the “less than 1 minute for 10K samples” requirement

  4. Practical deployment: Well-suited for production use in the web application

The performance validates the architectural choice of scikit-learn’s MLPRegressor for this 5-dimensional interpolation task.

Running Profiling¶

To reproduce these results:

cd backend
python profile_performance.py

Results are automatically saved to performance_results.json.

Fivedreg Interpolator
